revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 四所神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[渭東地区|福島]]2丁目3-34 |位置= |祭神= [[武甕槌命]]、[[斎王]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[比売神]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]]・[[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] |神事= }}
'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。また神社前にはこの地で育った[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられている。
== 概要 == 四所神社の起源は[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）と云われている（詳細は[[#伝承|伝承]]で）。[[三好郡]][[西祖谷山村]]（現[[三好市]]）の国見山山頂にある国見神社は奥社であるという説がある。
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]] - 歳日祭 * [[5月15日]] - 太々神楽祭 * [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]] - 夏祭 * [[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] - 秋祭（船だんじり）
== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定）
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市津田町の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている。<ref>[http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html 徳島新聞 お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る]</ref>
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市眉山町大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市南佐古町 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市伊賀町 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市勢見町
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称= 四所神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[渭東地区|福島]]2丁目3-34 |位置= |祭神= [[武甕槌命]]、[[斎王]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[比売神]] |社格= |創建= |本殿= |例祭= [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]]・[[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] |神事= }}
'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 概要 == 四所神社の起源は[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）と云われている（詳細は[[#伝承|伝承]]で）。徳島市中五社のひとつ。[[三好郡]][[西祖谷山村]]（現[[三好市]]）の国見山山頂にある国見神社は奥社であるという説がある。また神社前にはこの地で育った[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられている。
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]] - 歳日祭 * [[5月15日]] - 太々神楽祭 * [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]] - 夏祭 * [[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] - 秋祭（船だんじり）
== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定） * 神社前には[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられ、周辺には[[東照寺 (徳島市)|東照寺]]・[[慈光寺 (徳島市)|慈光寺]]等の寺院がある。
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市津田町の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている。<ref>[http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html 徳島新聞 お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る]</ref>
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市眉山町大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市南佐古町 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市伊賀町 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市勢見町
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。
== 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] * [[渭東地区]] * [[徳島県道29号徳島環状線]] - 県道沿いに位置。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[三好市]]（旧[[三好郡]][[西祖谷山村]]）の[[国見山 (徳島県)|国見山]]山頂にある国見神社は奥社であるという説がある。また神社前にはこの地で育った[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられている。[[海部ハナ]]ゆかりの神社としても知られる。
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]] - 歳日祭 * [[5月15日]] - 太々神楽祭 * [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]] - 夏祭 * [[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] - 秋祭（船だんじり）
== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定） * 神社前には[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられ、周辺には[[東照寺 (徳島市)|東照寺]]・[[慈光寺 (徳島市)|慈光寺]]等の寺院がある。
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市[[津田町 (徳島市)|津田町]]の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている。<ref>[http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html 徳島新聞 お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る]</ref>
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。 * [[徳島市営バス]][[沖洲村|沖洲]]・[[南海フェリー]]前行き「福島明神前」下車すぐ。
== 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] * [[渭東地区]] * [[徳島県道29号徳島環状線]] - 県道沿いに位置。
== 脚注 == <references />

== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定） * 神社前には[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられ、周辺には[[東照寺 (徳島市)|東照寺]]・[[慈光寺 (徳島市)|慈光寺]]等の寺院がある。 <gallery> 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|四所神社の境内の様子 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|ふくろうの樹 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|四所神社の案内番 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|四所神社の本殿 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（表） 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（裏） </gallery>
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市[[津田町 (徳島市)|津田町]]の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |title=お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る |work=徳島新聞 |date=2008-06-16 |accessdate=2009-10-11 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101017044927/https://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |archivedate=2010-10-17}}</ref>。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。 * [[徳島市営バス]][[沖洲村|沖洲]]・[[南海フェリー]]前行き「福島明神前」下車すぐ。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] * [[渭東地区]] * [[徳島県道29号徳島環状線]] - 県道沿いに位置。

revision.comment: 近年見つかった、四所神社の龍蛇神について追記しました。当方は四所神社神職の辻です。
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 四所神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] | 所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]2丁目3-34 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 19.4 | 経度度 = 134 | 経度分 = 34 | 経度秒 = 10.9 | 祭神 = [[武甕槌命]]、[[斎王]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[比売神]] | 社格 = [[旧郷社]] | 創建 = [[807年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年） | 本殿 = | 例祭 = [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]]・[[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Tokushima city }}
'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[とくしま市民遺産]]選定。
== 概要 == 四所神社の起源は[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）と云われている（詳細は[[#伝承|伝承]]で）。徳島市中五社のひとつ。
古くは四所明神・四所大明神と称し、現在でも福島の明神さんとして親しまれている。
「四所神社には蛇が住んでいる」という言い伝えがあったが、その詳細を知る人はおらず、伝承が失われる寸前であった。近年発見された古文書から、出雲大社の御神宝、龍蛇神が二体祀られていることが発覚した。
天明8年（1788年）、出雲大社から徳島藩へ龍蛇神が納められた。徳島藩はこれを畏み、四所神社に納めた。以後は参勤交代の際に、藩主が乗る御座船に祀って御祈祷を行った。
その2年後の寛政2年（1790年）、阿波水軍総大将の森甚五兵衛が、出雲から持ち帰った龍蛇神を四所神社に納めた。以前藩主から納められた龍蛇神は男で、この度のものは女であることから、夫婦が揃いまことに不思議なことだ、と『続阿波国徴古雑抄』に記されている。
これを受けて、令和6年（2024年）には龍蛇神祭が斎行された。
神社前にはこの地で育った[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられている。[[海部ハナ]]ゆかりの神社としても知られる。
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]] - 歳日祭 * [[5月15日]] - 太々神楽祭 * [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]] - 夏祭 * [[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] - 秋祭（船だんじり）
== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定） * 神社前には[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられ、周辺には[[東照寺 (徳島市)|東照寺]]・[[慈光寺 (徳島市)|慈光寺]]等の寺院がある。 <gallery> 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|四所神社の境内の様子 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|ふくろうの樹 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|四所神社の案内番 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|四所神社の本殿 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（表） 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（裏） </gallery>
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市[[津田町 (徳島市)|津田町]]の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |title=お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る |work=徳島新聞 |date=2008-06-16 |accessdate=2009-10-11 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101017044927/https://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |archivedate=2010-10-17}}</ref>。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。 * [[徳島市営バス]][[沖洲村|沖洲]]・[[南海フェリー]]前行き「福島明神前」下車すぐ。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] * [[渭東地区]] * [[徳島県道29号徳島環状線]] - 県道沿いに位置。
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html 徳島市：とくしま市民遺産 四所神社の船だんじり]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |Image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |Location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

{{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |Image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |Location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - {{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō {{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

{{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

{{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}[[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]]

It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō {{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |Coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |Shrine Rank= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |Annual Festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Shrine |Name= Shisho Shrine |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō
