{{Shrine |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō {{Shrine |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |Deities= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}

{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young prankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == *
