Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|12=draft|12=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|12=draft|12=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]]

A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }}[[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:四所神社 (徳島市) up to rev 100843260
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 四所神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] | 所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]2丁目3-34 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 19.4 | 経度度 = 134 | 経度分 = 34 | 経度秒 = 10.9 | 祭神 = [[武甕槌命]]、[[斎王]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[比売神]] | 社格 = [[旧郷社]] | 創建 = [[807年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年） | 本殿 = | 例祭 = [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]]・[[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Tokushima city }}
'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[とくしま市民遺産]]選定。
== 概要 == 四所神社の起源は[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）と云われている（詳細は[[#伝承|伝承]]で）。徳島市中五社のひとつ。
古くは四所明神・四所大明神と称し、現在でも福島の明神さんとして親しまれている。
「四所神社には蛇が住んでいる」という言い伝えがあったが、その詳細を知る人はおらず、伝承が失われる寸前であった。近年発見された古文書から、出雲大社の御神宝、龍蛇神が二体祀られていることが発覚した。
天明8年（1788年）、出雲大社から徳島藩へ龍蛇神が納められた。徳島藩はこれを畏み、四所神社に納めた。以後は参勤交代の際に、藩主が乗る御座船に祀って御祈祷を行った。
その2年後の寛政2年（1790年）、阿波水軍総大将の森甚五兵衛が、出雲から持ち帰った龍蛇神を四所神社に納めた。以前藩主から納められた龍蛇神は男で、この度のものは女であることから、夫婦が揃いまことに不思議なことだ、と『続阿波国徴古雑抄』に記されている。
これを受けて、令和6年（2024年）には龍蛇神祭が斎行された。
神社前にはこの地で育った[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられている。[[海部ハナ]]ゆかりの神社としても知られる。
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]] - 歳日祭 * [[5月15日]] - 太々神楽祭 * [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]] - 夏祭 * [[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] - 秋祭（船だんじり）
== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定） * 神社前には[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられ、周辺には[[東照寺 (徳島市)|東照寺]]・[[慈光寺 (徳島市)|慈光寺]]等の寺院がある。 <gallery> 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|四所神社の境内の様子 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|ふくろうの樹 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|四所神社の案内番 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|四所神社の本殿 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（表） 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（裏） </gallery>
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市[[津田町 (徳島市)|津田町]]の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |title=お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る |work=徳島新聞 |date=2008-06-16 |accessdate=2009-10-11 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101017044927/https://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |archivedate=2010-10-17}}</ref>。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。 * [[徳島市営バス]][[沖洲村|沖洲]]・[[南海フェリー]]前行き「福島明神前」下車すぐ。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] * [[渭東地区]] * [[徳島県道29号徳島環状線]] - 県道沿いに位置。
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html 徳島市：とくしま市民遺産 四所神社の船だんじり]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[四所神社 (徳島市)]] to [[Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 四所神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] | 所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]2丁目3-34 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 19.4 | 経度度 = 134 | 経度分 = 34 | 経度秒 = 10.9 | 祭神 = [[武甕槌命]]、[[斎王]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[比売神]] | 社格 = [[旧郷社]] | 創建 = [[807年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年） | 本殿 = | 例祭 = [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]]・[[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Tokushima city }}
'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[とくしま市民遺産]]選定。
== 概要 == 四所神社の起源は[[大同 (日本)|大同]]2年（[[807年]]）と云われている（詳細は[[#伝承|伝承]]で）。徳島市中五社のひとつ。
古くは四所明神・四所大明神と称し、現在でも福島の明神さんとして親しまれている。
「四所神社には蛇が住んでいる」という言い伝えがあったが、その詳細を知る人はおらず、伝承が失われる寸前であった。近年発見された古文書から、出雲大社の御神宝、龍蛇神が二体祀られていることが発覚した。
天明8年（1788年）、出雲大社から徳島藩へ龍蛇神が納められた。徳島藩はこれを畏み、四所神社に納めた。以後は参勤交代の際に、藩主が乗る御座船に祀って御祈祷を行った。
その2年後の寛政2年（1790年）、阿波水軍総大将の森甚五兵衛が、出雲から持ち帰った龍蛇神を四所神社に納めた。以前藩主から納められた龍蛇神は男で、この度のものは女であることから、夫婦が揃いまことに不思議なことだ、と『続阿波国徴古雑抄』に記されている。
これを受けて、令和6年（2024年）には龍蛇神祭が斎行された。
神社前にはこの地で育った[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられている。[[海部ハナ]]ゆかりの神社としても知られる。
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]] - 歳日祭 * [[5月15日]] - 太々神楽祭 * [[7月8日]]・[[7月9日]] - 夏祭 * [[10月27日]]・[[10月28日]] - 秋祭（船だんじり）
== 境内 == * 天神社 * 醫王神社 * ふくろうの樹 - 徳島市指定保存樹木第一号の[[マツ|松]]（[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月11日]]に指定） * 神社前には[[小説家]]・[[海野十三]]文学碑が建てられ、周辺には[[東照寺 (徳島市)|東照寺]]・[[慈光寺 (徳島市)|慈光寺]]等の寺院がある。 <gallery> 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|四所神社の境内の様子 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|ふくろうの樹 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|四所神社の案内番 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|四所神社の本殿 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（表） 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|四所神社にある海野十三の碑（裏） </gallery>
== 伝承 == 昔、徳島市[[津田町 (徳島市)|津田町]]の沖合に亀島と呼ばれる島が存在し、島内にある祠には二頭の[[シカ]]が飾られていた。ここに毎日お参りに訪れていた老夫婦の夢に神様が現れ、「シカの目が赤くなったら島が沈むので、シカを連れて逃げなさい」と言った。この話を聞いた若者がいたずらでシカの目を赤く塗った為、老夫婦は急いでシカを船に乗せて逃げた。しかしその晩、本当に大津波が島を襲い島は沈んでしまった。その後、老夫婦は自分たちを救ってくれたのはシカのおかげだと思い、シカを神社に祀ったのが四所神社の起こりだと云われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |title=お亀磯伝説 シカ津波から夫婦守る |work=徳島新聞 |date=2008-06-16 |accessdate=2009-10-11 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101017044927/https://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |archivedate=2010-10-17}}</ref>。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より車で約5分。 * [[徳島市営バス]][[沖洲村|沖洲]]・[[南海フェリー]]前行き「福島明神前」下車すぐ。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] * [[渭東地区]] * [[徳島県道29号徳島環状線]] - 県道沿いに位置。

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }}[[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}}

An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[sn: failed]]

An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sisyo-jinja.jpg|240px]] |location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chome 3-34 |coordinates= |deity= {{ill|Takemikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type= {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[sn: failed]]

{{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}} |type= {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|旧郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} |Established= 807 (Daido 2) |Main Hall= |festival= {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8|qq=}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 9|qq=}}, {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 27|qq=}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 28|qq=}} |Rituals= |Map= Japan Tokushima city }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Fukushima (Tokushima City)|ja|福島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima (Tokushima City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}. It has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima Citizen Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Citizen Heritage|qq=}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (see {{ill|Legend|ja|伝承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legend|qq=}}). It is one of the five notable shrines of central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, the shrine is affectionately called "Fukushima Myojin" by locals. There is a legend that a serpent resides within the shrine, though the details were nearly forgotten. Recently discovered ancient documents revealed that two sacred {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon Serpents|qq=}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the {{ill|Dragon Serpents|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon Serpents|qq=}} were delivered to the Tokushima Domain from Izumo Taisha. Revering their divine power, the domain enshrined them in Shisho Shrine, where they were venerated during ceremonial voyages. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), another Dragon Serpent brought by the naval commander Mori Jingobei was also enshrined. It was recorded in ''Zoku Awa Kuni Choko Zassho'' that the first serpent was male, while the second was female, forming a mystical pair. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the shrine began holding the {{ill|Dragon Serpent Festival|ja|龍蛇神祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon Serpent Festival|qq=}}. The shrine grounds include a literary monument dedicated to the novelist {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unno Juza|qq=}}, who grew up in the area. The shrine is also connected to {{ill|Hana Kaifu|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Kaifu|qq=}}. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}} - New Year Festival * {{ill|May 15|ja|5月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 15|qq=}} - Taidai Kagura Festival * {{ill|July 8|ja|7月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8|qq=}}, {{ill|July 9|ja|7月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 9|qq=}} - Summer Festival * {{ill|October 27|ja|10月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 27|qq=}}, {{ill|October 28|ja|10月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 28|qq=}} - Autumn Festival (with ''Chōsa'' portable shrine) == Grounds == * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Iō Shrine|ja|醫王神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iō Shrine|qq=}} * '''Tree of Owls''' - A pine tree designated as Tokushima City’s first protected tree in 1974 (Showa 49). * The shrine grounds include a literary monument for {{ill|Unno Juza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unno Juza|qq=}}. Nearby landmarks include {{ill|Toshoji (Tokushima)|ja|東照寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshoji (Tokushima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Jikōji (Tokushima)|ja|慈光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōji (Tokushima)|qq=}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|View of the Shisho Shrine grounds 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Tree of Owls 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board at Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (front) 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Monument to Unno Juza (back) == Legend == A legend recounts that in the offshore area of Tokushima City's {{ill|Tsuda Town|ja|津田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuda Town|qq=}}, there was once an island called Kamejima, where a shrine adorned with two deer statues stood. An elderly couple visited the shrine daily until they dreamt of a deity warning, "When the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink; take the deer and flee." A young pRankster painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to flee with the statues. That night, a great tsunami submerged the island. Grateful for their rescue, the couple enshrined the deer at Shisho Shrine. == Central Tokushima Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}} Ōtakiyama * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako 3rd Avenue|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Iga Town|ja|伊賀町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima City {{ill|Seimi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Town|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop on {{ill|Tokushima City Bus|ja|徳島市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Bus|qq=}} routes to Okisumura and Nankai Ferry. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Itō District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō District|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Road 29|qq=}} - Located along the prefectural road. == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Citizen Heritage - Shisho Shrine Boat Floats] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[ja:%E5%9B%9B%E6%89%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%BE%B3%E5%B3%B6%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市福島)]] [[sn:hisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)]] [[sn: failed]] ==Merged second translation== '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is recognized as part of the {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}. == Overview == The origins of Shisho Shrine date back to 807 (Daido 2), according to tradition (details are provided under [[#Legend|Legend]]). It is one of the five shrines in central Tokushima. Historically known as Shisho Myojin or Shisho Daimyojin, it remains affectionately referred to as "Fukushima's Myojin-san" by locals. A long-standing legend mentions that "a snake resides in Shisho Shrine," though the details were nearly lost. Recently discovered historical documents reveal that two {{ill|Dragon-snake deities|ja|龍蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon-snake deities|qq=}} from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} are enshrined here. In 1788 (Tenmei 8), the Tokushima Domain received a dragon-snake deity from Izumo Taisha and enshrined it in Shisho Shrine. During the domain lord's sankin-kōtai processions, the deity was carried aboard his ceremonial ship for blessings. Two years later, in 1790 (Kansei 2), General Mori Jingoemon of the Awa Navy brought another dragon-snake deity from Izumo. The earlier deity was male, and the new one was female, forming a pair. This unusual occurrence is documented in the *Zoku Awa-koku Choko Zassho*. In 2024 (Reiwa 6), the Dragon-Snake Deity Festival was conducted to honor this history. A literary monument for the novelist {{ill|Unno Jūza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unno Jūza|qq=}}, a local native, stands in front of the shrine. It is also associated with {{ill|Kaifu Hana|ja|海部ハナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaifu Hana|qq=}}. == Festivals == * [[January 1]] - New Year’s Festival (Saijitsusai) * [[May 15]] - Grand Kagura Festival * [[July 8]]–[[July 9]] - Summer Festival * [[October 27]]–[[October 28]] - Autumn Festival (Ship Danjiri) == Grounds == * '''Ten Shrine''' (天神社) * '''Iō Shrine''' (醫王神社) * '''Fukuro Tree''': A pine tree designated as the first municipal protected tree of Tokushima City in 1974 (Showa 49). * A literary monument to {{ill|Unno Jūza|ja|海野十三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unno Jūza|qq=}} stands on the shrine grounds. Nearby, there are temples such as {{ill|Tōshōji|ja|東照寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōshōji|qq=}} and {{ill|Jikōji|ja|慈光寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōji|qq=}}. 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 1.jpg|Shrine grounds of Shisho Shrine 四所神社 (徳島市) ふくろうの樹.jpg|Fukuro Tree 四所神社 (徳島市) 案内板.jpg|Information board 四所神社 (徳島市) 境内 3.jpg|Main hall 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 表.jpg|Front of Unno Jūza monument 四所神社 (徳島市) 海野十三碑 裏.jpg|Back of Unno Jūza monument == Legend == In the past, an island called Kamejima existed off the coast of {{ill|Tsuda Town, Tokushima City|ja|津田町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuda Town, Tokushima City|qq=}}. A shrine on the island featured two decorated deer. An elderly couple who visited daily dreamed that the shrine deity warned them, "If the deer’s eyes turn red, the island will sink. Take the deer and flee." A mischievous youth painted the deer’s eyes red, prompting the couple to escape with the deer by boat. That night, a massive tsunami submerged the island. Believing their rescue was thanks to the deer, the couple enshrined them at Shisho Shrine. This story is recounted in local lore.{{Cite web|url=http://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |title=Legend of Kameiso: Deer Protect Elderly Couple from Tsunami |work=Tokushima Shimbun |date=2008-06-16 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101017044927/https://www.topics.or.jp/nie/117307666665/121360160271.html |archivedate=2010-10-17}} == Central Five Shrines of Tokushima == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Bizan, Tokushima * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in Minamisako, Tokushima * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in Iga, Tokushima * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in Semimachi, Tokushima == Access == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from [[Tokushima Station]] (JR). * Near "Fukushima Myojin-mae" bus stop, accessible via [[Tokushima City Bus]] or [[Nankai Ferry]] routes. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ito District|ja|渭東地区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito District|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Route 29|ja|徳島県道29号徳島環状線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Route 29|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.tokushima.tokushima.jp/kankou/shimin_isan/history/022.html Tokushima City: Shisho Shrine and Ship Danjiri] {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|version=100843260|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[ja:四所神社 (徳島市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)]] [[Category:Merged pages]]
